steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage ends with "Thus, ______" — the blank must be a logical conclusion drawn from what was just said. The key evidence: biophilic architects "carefully contemplate every aspect of their projects from location characteristics and initial materials selection to ultimate interior design choices and building installation." This covers the full arc from the earliest planning phase through final execution.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'biophilic design considerations are exercised during both the conception and execution of a new project.': Directly mirrors the passage — "from initial materials selection" (conception) "to building installation" (execution). This is the conclusion the "thus" signals. ✓
  • Choice B — 'architecture firms like Young Projects typically work with large crews in order to expedite the time it takes to build a new project.': Crew size and speed are never mentioned in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — 'architecture firms like Young Projects aim to use unique building materials for each project that they design.': The passage mentions selecting materials but says nothing about uniqueness. ✗
  • Choice D — 'biophilic design prioritizes the emotional effects on inhabitants rather than the physical state of the natural surroundings.': The passage describes a balanced "physical and emotional relationship" — claiming emotional effects are prioritized over physical ones contradicts the text. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
